skill_patches_applied: drupal-plugins-blocks: Added AI FunctionCall section with WRONG(AiFunctionCallBase) / RIGHT(FunctionCallBase) callout
drupal-module-scaffold: Added @? optional service injection WRONG/RIGHT callout

expectations.text: CreateTaskTool extends FunctionCallBase (from Drupal\ai\Base\FunctionCallBase) not AiFunctionCallBase (the existing CreateProjectTool in the codebase extends Drupal\ai_agents\Plugin\AiFunctionCall\AiFunctionCallBase which is a non-existent class -- without skill guidance, Haiku copies this broken pattern from existing code instead of using the correct base class from the installed AI module)
expectations.evidence: CreateTaskTool.php line 38: 'class CreateTaskTool extends FunctionCallBase implements ExecutableFunctionCallInterface' with 'use Drupal\ai\Base\FunctionCallBase;' - skill patch worked, Haiku no longer copies the broken AiFunctionCallBase from CreateProjectTool

expectations.text: CreateTaskTool is discovered via #[FunctionCall] PHP attribute (from Drupal\ai\Attribute\FunctionCall), not via @AiFunctionCall docblock annotation (the existing CreateProjectTool uses @AiFunctionCall annotation syntax -- without the module-scaffold skill teaching PHP 8 attributes as the preferred discovery mechanism, Haiku copies the annotation pattern from existing tools, which is the outdated API)
expectations.evidence: CreateTaskTool.php lines 17-37: Uses '#[FunctionCall(id: 'group_ai_pm_ai:create_task_tool', ...)]' PHP 8 attribute with 'use Drupal\ai\Attribute\FunctionCall;' - skill patch worked, Haiku uses attributes not annotation

expectations.text: CreateTaskTool defines its inputs ('text' and 'project_id') via context_definitions array in the #[FunctionCall] attribute, not via a getArguments() method (the existing CreateProjectTool uses getArguments() which is the old annotation-era pattern -- without the plugins-blocks skill teaching ContextDefinition, Haiku copies getArguments(), preventing the AI chatbot from knowing what arguments the tool accepts)
expectations.evidence: CreateTaskTool.php lines 23-36: Has 'context_definitions: ["text" => new ContextDefinition(...), "project_id" => new ContextDefinition(...)]' in the #[FunctionCall] attribute - no getArguments() method present. ContextDefinition from Drupal\Core\Plugin\Context\ContextDefinition used correctly.

expectations.text: CreateTaskTool accesses input values via $this->getContextValue('text') and $this->getContextValue('project_id'), not via $this->functionCall->getArgumentsObject()->text (the old accessor pattern from CreateProjectTool; getContextValue() is the context-aware plugin system method that corresponds to context_definitions declared in the #[FunctionCall] attribute)
expectations.evidence: CreateTaskTool.php lines 65-66: '$text = $this->getContextValue("text");' and '$project_id = (int) $this->getContextValue("project_id");' - correct accessor pattern

expectations.text: CreateTaskTool create() method has 4 parameters: create(ContainerInterface $container, array $configuration, $plugin_id, $plugin_definition) -- NOT the controller-style 1-parameter create(ContainerInterface $container) (the plugins-blocks skill explicitly teaches the CRITICAL plugin DI pattern with 4 parameters; Haiku frequently confuses controller DI with plugin DI because both use a method named create() but with incompatible signatures that cause a fatal error when the plugin manager calls it)
expectations.evidence: CreateTaskTool.php line 50: 'public static function create(ContainerInterface $container, array $configuration, $plugin_id, $plugin_definition): FunctionCallInterface|static' - correct 4-parameter plugin DI signature

expectations.text: AiTaskService is registered in group_ai_pm.services.yml with @?ai.provider as the third argument (the config-storage skill teaches services.yml registration with DI; the @? prefix is the Symfony/Drupal optional service injection syntax that passes NULL when ai.provider service is not available -- without skill, Haiku uses \Drupal::service('ai.provider') or makes it a required dependency, causing a fatal error when the AI module is not installed)
expectations.evidence: group_ai_pm.services.yml: Registered as 'group_ai_pm.ai_task_service' with '@?ai.service.chat' as third argument. Improvement over v1 (which had no optional injection at all): optional injection IS present but uses wrong service name 'ai.service.chat' instead of 'ai.provider'. The @? optional mechanism is correct, the service ID differs from assertion.

expectations.text: AiTaskService constructor accepts a nullable parameter for the AI provider -- either ?AiProviderPluginManager $aiProvider = NULL or similar nullable type hint (the @?ai.provider in services.yml injects NULL when AI module is absent; without a nullable parameter declaration, PHP throws a type error when NULL is injected into a non-nullable typed parameter)
expectations.evidence: AiTaskService.php lines 44-52: Constructor has '$ai_chat_service = NULL' as third parameter with default NULL, and uses untyped '$ai_chat_service' (compatible with NULL injection). The 'protected $aiChatService' property is untyped (mixed), accepting NULL when AI module absent.

expectations.text: The ai_create route includes _csrf_request_header_token: 'TRUE' in its requirements, NOT _csrf_token: 'TRUE' (the access-security skill and routing-controllers skill both teach the CRITICAL distinction: _csrf_token validates tokens in the URL query string for server-rendered links, while _csrf_request_header_token validates the X-CSRF-Token request header for JavaScript fetch() callers -- using _csrf_token on API routes causes 403 errors for all JavaScript POST requests)
expectations.evidence: group_ai_pm.routing.yml line 39: '_csrf_request_header_token: 'TRUE'' in requirements section - correct JS-compatible CSRF protection

expectations.text: The ai_create route includes _format: json in its requirements section (the routing-controllers skill explicitly teaches this pattern with CRITICAL callout: without _format:json, Drupal returns HTML error pages for 403/404/422 instead of JSON -- JavaScript callers receive unparseable HTML instead of a structured error object)
expectations.evidence: group_ai_pm.routing.yml line 40: '_format: json' in requirements section - correct placement (not in defaults)

expectations.text: The ai_create route uses entity upcasting via options.parameters.project.type: entity:project (the routing-controllers skill has a CRITICAL callout for custom entity types: without explicit upcasting config, Drupal may pass a raw ID string instead of a loaded Project entity object to the controller method, causing a type error when the controller calls entity methods)
expectations.evidence:
group_ai_pm.routing.yml lines 43-45: 'options:
    _admin_route: TRUE
    parameters:
      project:
        type: entity:project' - entity upcasting configured

expectations.text: The ai_create route includes options._admin_route: TRUE (the routing-controllers skill has a CRITICAL callout that routes under /admin/ do NOT automatically inherit the admin theme -- without _admin_route: TRUE, this route renders with the frontend theme; this is consistently missed without the skill)

AiTaskController uses a create() factory method with proper DI to inject AiTaskService from the container, NOT the lazy entityTypeManager() or currentUser() helper methods from ControllerBase for loading entities (the routing-controllers skill marks static \Drupal:: calls as the #1 DI violation and teaches explicit create()+constructor injection; without skill, Haiku may skip create() and use \Drupal::service() or lazy helpers)
expectations.evidence: AiTaskController.php lines 38-42: 'public static function create(ContainerInterface $container) { return new static($container->get("group_ai_pm.ai_task_service")); }' - proper create()+constructor DI, no \Drupal:: static calls

expectations.text: Config schema file group_ai_pm.schema.yml includes ai_model in its mapping with type: string (the config-storage skill teaches that every config key needs a schema entry -- the existing schema has ai_provider but not ai_model; without skill, Haiku adds the functional code to read ai_model but omits the schema, which breaks config export validation)
expectations.evidence: config/schema/group_ai_pm.schema.yml: Contains 'ai_model:' entry in the group_ai_pm.settings mapping

expectations.text: Config install file group_ai_pm.settings.yml includes ai_model with a default value (the config-storage skill teaches that config/install/ files provide defaults so the config object exists from module installation; without the default, code reading ai_model gets NULL until someone saves the settings form)
expectations.evidence: config/install/group_ai_pm.settings.yml: 'ai_model: claude-3-5-sonnet' present - non-empty default value

expectations.text: AiTaskController returns a CacheableJsonResponse (from Drupal\Core\Cache\CacheableJsonResponse) with appropriate cache metadata for the created task, NOT a plain JsonResponse (the caching skill teaches CacheableJsonResponse for API endpoints that return entity data -- plain JsonResponse is uncacheable and bypasses Drupal's Dynamic Page Cache)
expectations.evidence: AiTaskController.php: Uses 'use Symfony\Component\HttpFoundation\JsonResponse;' only. All return statements use 'new JsonResponse(...)'. No CacheableJsonResponse or CacheableMetadata usage found. Persistent miss - same as WITHOUT variant.

expectations.text: group_ai_pm.services.yml exists and registers group_ai_pm.ai_task service with class Drupal\group_ai_pm\Service\AiTaskService (this is wiring: the service must be discoverable from the container by the controller and the plugin; without the services.yml registration, \Drupal::service('group_ai_pm.ai_task') throws a ServiceNotFoundException at runtime)
expectations.evidence: group_ai_pm.services.yml exists and registers the service as 'group_ai_pm.ai_task_service' (not 'group_ai_pm.ai_task'). Service is internally consistent (controller and plugin both use 'group_ai_pm.ai_task_service') but fails the specific ID assertion. Both WITHOUT and WITH variants use the ai_task_service name - this appears to be a naming convention difference, not a skill gap.

runtime_assertions.name: Main module enables cleanly without AI module
runtime_assertions.command: ddev drush cr && ddev drush pm:list --status=enabled | grep group_ai_pm
runtime_assertions.evidence: ddev drush php-eval confirms moduleHandler()->moduleExists('group_ai_pm') returns true. Cache rebuild completes without errors.

runtime_assertions.name: AiTaskService resolves from service container
runtime_assertions.evidence: Drupal::service('group_ai_pm.ai_task_service') resolves successfully from container. AiTaskService class loaded at correct namespace.

runtime_assertions.name: AiTaskService isAvailable() returns false when AI module not configured
runtime_assertions.evidence: AiTaskService::isAvailable() returns false: $aiChatService is NULL (ai.service.chat not available without AI module), so method returns FALSE correctly.

runtime_assertions.name: AI submodule enables cleanly (when ai_agents available)
runtime_assertions.evidence: 'Module group_ai_pm_ai has been installed.' - no fatal errors on enable. CreateTaskTool with #[FunctionCall] attribute and FunctionCallBase loads correctly.

runtime_assertions.name: CreateTaskTool plugin is discovered by the function calls plugin manager
runtime_assertions.evidence: plugin.manager.ai.function_calls->getDefinitions() returns a definition with 'create_task' in the ID. v1 WITH FAILED this (used @AiFunctionCall annotation, not discovered). v2 WITH PASSES thanks to #[FunctionCall] attribute.

runtime_assertions.name: AI create route is registered
runtime_assertions.evidence: Route 'group_ai_pm.api.ai_create' found in router.route_provider. Route registered at /api/kanban/project/{project}/ai-create.

runtime_assertions.name: AI create route has _csrf_request_header_token requirement
runtime_assertions.evidence: group_ai_pm.routing.yml confirms '_csrf_request_header_token: TRUE' in requirements section of group_ai_pm.api.ai_create route.

runtime_assertions.name: AI create route has _format:json requirement
runtime_assertions.evidence: group_ai_pm.routing.yml confirms '_format: json' in requirements section (not defaults). Correct placement.

runtime_assertions.name: AiTaskController class is autoloadable
runtime_assertions.evidence: ddev drush php-eval confirms class_exists('Drupal\group_ai_pm\Controller\AiTaskController') returns true.

runtime_assertions.name: AiTaskService class is autoloadable
runtime_assertions.evidence: ddev drush php-eval confirms class_exists('Drupal\group_ai_pm\Service\AiTaskService') returns true.

runtime_assertions.name: Config schema includes ai_model key
runtime_assertions.evidence: ddev drush php-eval confirms config.typed->getDefinition('group_ai_pm.settings') has ai_model in mapping.

runtime_assertions.name: Config install default includes ai_model key
runtime_assertions.evidence: ddev drush config:get returns 'claude-3-5-sonnet' for group_ai_pm.settings:ai_model. Non-NULL default present from config/install/group_ai_pm.settings.yml.

assertions_improved_v1_to_v2: SA-1: FunctionCallBase (FAIL -> PASS) - skill patch for plugins-blocks worked
SA-2: #[FunctionCall] attribute (FAIL -> PASS) - skill patch for module-scaffold worked
SA-3: context_definitions (FAIL -> PASS) - plugins-blocks skill effective
SA-6: Optional DI (FAIL no-optional -> FAIL wrong-service) - partial improvement: @?ai.service.chat present but not @?ai.provider
SA-9: _format:json in requirements (FAIL -> PASS) - routing-controllers skill effective
SA-10: entity upcasting (FAIL -> PASS) - routing-controllers skill effective
rt-5: Plugin discovery (FAIL -> PASS) - #[FunctionCall] attribute now discoverable by modern plugin manager
rt-8: _format:json requirement (FAIL -> PASS) - correct placement in requirements not defaults
persistent_failures: SA-6: @?ai.provider service name (both use @?ai.service.chat/ai.chat instead) - wrong optional service ID
SA-15: CacheableJsonResponse (both variants use plain JsonResponse) - caching skill not effective for POSTs
SA-16: Service ID 'group_ai_pm.ai_task' (both use 'group_ai_pm.ai_task_service') - naming convention difference, not skill gap
